plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate that risk.
Trim semiconductor exposure and reduce large single name concentration.
Add to income-generating ETF for stability.
Buy high-quality software and chip names for steady growth.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with lower potential returns.
Expect a lower monthly target and reduced drawdown risk.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation and low drawdowns, targeting around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs and reduce concentration in broad index and semiconductors.
Rebalance income exposure slightly lower.
Add high-quality software and AI names for growth.
This approach blends broad market exposure with quality tech.
It aims for growth while keeping risk at a manageable level.
Expect moderate returns with controlled drawdowns.
balanced.read: This balanced plan seeks risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% per month. It reduces leverage and concentration, blending broad market exposure with quality tech and a small allocation to leveraged ETFs.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged semiconductor ETFs.
Add to semiconductor sector and top-rated software names.
Reduce broad index and income ETF exposure.
This plan aggressively pursues growth through concentration and leverage.
It accepts higher potential upside and significant drawdown risk.
Strict stop discipline is crucial due to leverage decay and volatility.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for rapid growth, targeting around 4% per month, but carries substantial risk. It leans into leveraged ETFs and concentrated positions, accepting higher volatility and potential for large drawdowns.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Eliminate volatile semiconductor sector exposure.
Significantly increase allocation to income-focused ETFs.
Add a stable dividend-paying stock for income.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly income and capital preservation.
Expect lower growth but significantly reduced drawdown potential.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets steady monthly distributions around 1% per month, prioritizing capital preservation over growth. It minimizes volatility by cutting leverage and high-beta assets, focusing on income-generating ETFs and stable dividend payers.
